summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2021, conducting annual reorganization including electing Dan Houser as chairperson and Lisa Hodges as vice chairperson. The board approved a new columbarium program at Goshen Cemetery with fee schedules, accepted resignations of Officer Tim Budai (20 years) and Lieutenant Daryl Roberts (26 years) due to medical issues, approved the adjusted 2021 budget showing improved financial position with projected ending unencumbered funds of $1.9 million, and approved a 2021-2023 collective bargaining agreement with the firefighters union. A resident praised the board's proactive approach to addressing a nuisance property on Clarawell Drive that had been vacant for 21 years.

notable_topics: Annual reorganization: chairperson, vice chair, meeting schedule, compensation approvals
Columbarium program launch at Goshen Cemetery with fee schedule and rules
Resignation of Officer Tim Budai (20 years) and Lt. Daryl Roberts (26 years) for medical reasons
Nuisance property enforcement on Clarawell Drive (vacant 21 years)
Adjusted 2021 budget showing improved financial position

BZA and Zoning Commission vacancies need to be filled

notes: This was the annual reorganization meeting with many standard annual approvals. Laura Englund, fiscal officer, was absent. Executive session was held regarding employee compensation/appointments. The ORC requires trustees to be paid monthly, not biweekly as previously attempted. COVID relief funds were noted as 100% complete and partially used for the columbarium project. Officer Logan Weisenberg is a finalist for a police job in Florida. Captain Danny Vanover was approved for unpaid leave pending medical disability retirement. The board discussed forming a CIC-type economic development committee but tabled it for future discussion due to legal complexities from past CIC handling. Goshen Road Bridge repairs were underway with the county pushing replacement funding from 2023 to 2022. Gainer Road closure set for the 18th.
title: 1.12.2021 Board of Trustees Meeting
